meaning: n.啄痕,许多,配克(容量单位,等于2加仑)vt.啄食,扔vi.啄,吹毛求疵,啄食,扔石头v.<口>吃一点点
examples: The budgerigar gave a quick peck at the seed.
They thought the birds must have come to peck his flesh.
The sparrow perching upon the third horse, she began to peck him too.
Food and wine emporium Peck first opened its doors as a deli in 1883.
When I started to get out of the car with him, though, Peck balked.
